verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70/HSP72) is primarily a cytosolic chaperone lacking classical transmembrane domains or secretory signals. However, extensive evidence establishes a tumor-cell-selective surface form: membrane-bound HSPA1A is detected by flow cytometry on a wide variety of tumor cell lines and primary tumors using the cmHsp70.1 mAb that recognizes an extracellularly exposed epitope of the C-terminal domain. Surface localization is mediated by high-affinity binding to negatively charged phospholipids (phosphatidylserine, Gb3) in the outer leaflet, particularly in cholesterol-rich microdomains. This is tumor/stress-state-selective and not present on normal tissues screened. Thus, surface exposure occurs only under specific cell-state conditions (malignant transformation/stress), qualifying as contextual via cell_state_induced.
